runs.prompt:
TIMING PRIORITY RULE (APPLIES TO ALL CANDIDATES):
  3M return > 30% = automatically LATE TREND unless strong counter-evidence is present.
  LATE TREND = elevated pullback risk and lower entry quality.
  EARLY or MID TREND is always preferred over LATE TREND, even if momentum is weaker.

STRONG COUNTER-EVIDENCE DEFINITION:
  LATE TREND can only be overridden if ALL of the following are true:
    - Market verdict = RESEARCH NOW
    - Rotation = SUPPORT with score 80 or higher
    - No major contradictions in any signal
  If these conditions are not all met, LATE TREND must not be overridden.

LATE TREND OVERRIDE CLARIFICATION:
  Even if LATE TREND is overridden by strong counter-evidence,
  it must NOT be classified as BUY NOW.
  A LATE TREND override can only upgrade the entry from AVOID to WAIT.
  WAIT is the maximum allowed entry when timing is LATE TREND.

MAJOR SIGNAL CONFLICT DEFINITION:
  A major conflict exists if any of the following are true:
    - Market verdict is SKIP while Rotation is SUPPORT
    - Commodity trend contradicts sector direction
    - Earnings = FAIL or RISKY while price trend is strong
  Note: WATCH vs SUPPORT is a minor conflict, not a major one.
  Only the above conditions qualify as major conflicts.

BUY NOW CRITERIA (hard gate — all conditions must be true):
  BUY NOW is only allowed if ALL of the following are true:
    - Timing quality is EARLY TREND or MID TREND
    - Market verdict = RESEARCH NOW
    - Rotation = SUPPORT
    - entry_quality = GOOD (System 4 hard gate — no exceptions)
    - No major signal conflicts
  If entry_quality is EXTENDED or WEAK: BUY NOW is blocked regardless of all other signals.
  If entry_quality is EARLY: pattern unconfirmed — do not issue BUY NOW, use WAIT.
  If entry_quality is UNAVAILABLE: treat same as EARLY — reduce confidence, do not issue BUY NOW.
  If any other condition is not met, default to WAIT or AVOID.

DECISION HIERARCHY (use this when signals conflict):
  1. Timing quality (trend stage) has the highest priority.
  2. Market verdict (System 1) is the primary directional signal.
  3. Sector rotation confirms or weakens the timing.
  4. Earnings quality validates or blocks conviction.
  5. Price structure entry quality gates BUY NOW — cannot be overridden.

UNAVAILABLE DATA RULE:
  If earnings data is UNAVAILABLE, do not assume positive or negative.
  Reduce confidence level. Do not issue BUY NOW unless all other signals
  are strong and timing is EARLY or MID TREND.
  If price structure data is UNAVAILABLE, treat same as EARLY — reduce confidence,
  do not issue BUY NOW, verify entry manually.

COMMODITY CONTRADICTION RULE:
  If a candidate's sector depends on a commodity (e.g. energy stocks depend
  on oil/gas) and the commodity is falling while the stock is rising:
    - Treat this as a negative divergence
    - Reduce confidence level
    - Flag as a key risk in section 5

CONFIDENCE IN TIMING SCALE:
  LOW    -- conflicting signals or LATE TREND
  MEDIUM -- partial alignment or some uncertainty present
  HIGH   -- strong alignment across signals and EARLY or MID TREND

Do not use certainty words: never say "will", "guaranteed", or "certain".
Always prioritize downside risk before upside potential.
If signals conflict, highlight the conflict clearly before drawing any conclusion.

=============================================================
SYSTEM CONTEXT — READ THIS BEFORE ANALYZING
=============================================================

This report was generated by an automated stock screening system.
The system has four scoring layers:

MARKET SIGNAL (System 1)
  Confidence score 0-100. Primary ranking signal.
  Market verdict vocabulary:
    RESEARCH NOW — strong setup, multiple signals aligned
    WATCH        — moderate setup, signal not fully confirmed
    SKIP         — weak or conflicting setup, do not act

EARNINGS QUALITY (System 2)
  EQ Score 0-100. Measures earnings reliability.
  Pass tier vocabulary:
    PASS (SUPPORTIVE) — earnings are reliable and cash-backed
    WATCH (NEUTRAL)   — earnings are acceptable but have concerns
    FAIL (WEAK)       — earnings are unreliable
    fatal flaw → RISKY — overrides all tiers, critical structural problem
    UNAVAILABLE       — no SEC data found for this ticker

SECTOR ROTATION (System 3)
  Rotation score 0-100. Measures sector timing.
  Signal vocabulary:
    SUPPORT  — sector flow supports acting now
    WAIT     — neutral timing, not yet favorable
    WEAKEN   — sector flow is deteriorating
    UNKNOWN  — insufficient data

PRICE STRUCTURE (System 4)
  Price action score 0-100. Measures entry timing quality using OHLCV only.
  entry_quality vocabulary (hard gate — controls BUY NOW eligibility):
    GOOD      — price near support or confirmed base, valid entry window
    EXTENDED  — price far above support, elevated pullback risk, do not enter
    EARLY     — pattern forming but not yet confirmed, wait for confirmation
    WEAK      — no valid setup detected, avoid entry
    UNAVAILABLE — insufficient OHLCV data
  key_level_position vocabulary:
    NEAR_SUPPORT     — price close to a support zone
    MID_RANGE        — price between support and resistance
    NEAR_RESISTANCE  — price approaching a resistance zone
    BREAKOUT         — price breaking above resistance
  structure_state vocabulary: TRENDING / CONSOLIDATING / VOLATILE

COMBINED READING (four-layer synthesis)
  Alignment vocabulary:
    ALIGNED  — all four systems agree
    PARTIAL  — mixed signals, partial agreement
    CONFLICT — systems disagree

TIMING QUALITY DEFINITIONS (use these when classifying)
  EARLY TREND  — price up less than 15% in 3 months, momentum building
  MID TREND    — price up 15-30% in 3 months, trend established
  LATE TREND   — price up more than 30% in 3 months, elevated pullback risk
